name: Castillete de Fayón — trail running circular desde Nonaspe
region_intro: Jogging around Nonaspe offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its river valleys and surrounding hills. The region provides a mix of terrain, from paths alongside the Matarraña River to trails with moderate ascents. Runners can explore the natural environment, which includes varied vegetation and open views. Best jogging routes around Nonaspe The most popular jogging route is Castillete de Fayón — trail running circular desde Nonaspe, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a circular path with some elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is View of the Matarraña River loop from la Pobla de Massaluca, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Matarraña River and includes significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Running loop from Batea, a 3.9 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 41 minutes. Jogging around Nonaspe is defined by river loops, trail running opportunities, and routes with moderate hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to more challenging trails. The routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Nonaspe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Nonaspe?
A: There are 9 dedicated running routes around Nonaspe, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. You'll find 7 moderate routes and 2 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Nonaspe?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy', many of the moderate routes can be suitable for beginners looking to challenge themselves. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Batea&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.9-mile (6.4 km) trail that can be completed in about 41 minutes, offering a good introduction to the region's terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Nonaspe?
A: The running trails in Nonaspe feature a diverse landscape, characterized by river valleys and surrounding hills. You can expect a mix of paths alongside the Matarraña River, as well as trails with moderate ascents and varied vegetation, providing open views of the natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Nonaspe are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983245781/castillete-de-fayon-trail-running-circular-desde-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillete de Fayón — trail running circular desde Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.8-mile (14.2 km) moderate trail.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks I can see while running near Nonaspe?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can enjoy views of the Ebro River at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4601307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mequinenza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6243829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic sections for runners.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Nonaspe?
A: The running routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Over 60 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic river loops and the opportunities for trail running with moderate hills.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Matarraña River loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.6-mile (17.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and offers expansive views of the Matarraña River.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of running routes in Nonaspe?
A: Running routes in Nonaspe vary in length and duration. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Batea&lt;/a&gt; at 3.9 miles (6.4 km) taking about 41 minutes, to longer trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de Valljordà loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 miles (11.6 km) and typically takes around 1 hour 15 minutes.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer views of lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Nonaspe area is close to several impressive bodies of water. You can find routes that offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5821907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mequinenza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing beautiful waterside scenery during your run.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river paths?
A: Yes, jogging around Nonaspe is notably defined by its river loops. Many routes offer paths alongside the Matarraña River, providing a refreshing and scenic running experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Matarraña River loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering continuous river views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Nonaspe?
A: Nonaspe generally experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal for running, with milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler.
tips: Nice picture from the path of the Algás river as it passes through Nonaspe.

Located at the top of the hill, about seven kilometers from the town of Nonaspe, we find this building that was built as a watchtower during the third Carlist war in the 19th century to dominate the Matarraña valley.
At this point is the monument to the "Bombers of the Generalitat de Catalunya" who lost their lives in the fire of September 14, 1994.

waypoints.name: Monument in Memory of the Firefighters of the Generalitat de Catalunya (1994 Fire)
